page.title: Farming Dashboard
fields.title: Field Management
fields.subtitle: Live field data — refreshes every 5 seconds
fields.refreshRulesTitle: Re-run offline rules on current field data
fields.waitingDataBody: Make sure FS25 is running with the Farm Dashboard mod active.
fields.apiErrorBody: Make sure FS25 is running and the FarmDashboard mod is active.
fields.clusterMerged: Merged view — {{count}} parcels. Soil bars use the lead parcel (#{{lead}}).
fields.balesOnFieldTitle: Bales counted on this farmland (game items)
fields.windrowBadgeTitle: Tipped material on field (height-map total from mod)
fields.looseStrawTitle: Loose straw detected on field samples
fields.grassWindrowTitle: Grass windrow detected on field samples
fields.hayWindrowTitle: Hay / dry grass windrow detected on field samples
fields.baleLooseTitle: Loose material a baler can pick up (height-map)
fields.windrowAnyTitle: Any loose fill on field samples (includes crop swaths)
fields.winterSeasonNote: Winter: arable growth is minimal — field readings may look unchanged until spring. Suggestions still reflect soil prep and planning.
fields.hectaresUnavailableTitle: Area is supplied when Farming Simulator is running and the dashboard mod can read field geometry. If the game is closed, area may be unavailable.
tools.shopNeedRole: {{label}} — shop if you need this type for the job
tools.typicalToolsFallback: Typical tools: match the job to the right shop category (implements / harvesters / spreaders) for this field state.
vehicles.title: Vehicle Fleet Management
vehicles.subtitle: Monitor your vehicle fleet, fuel levels, and maintenance status
vehicles.emptyNoneBody: No vehicles are currently available in your farm.
vehicles.emptyStorageBody: Only storage items (pallets/bigBags) are available. These are hidden from vehicle display.
economy.title: Economic Dashboard
economy.subtitle: Monitor your finances, purchases, and market prices
economy.consumablesHelp: Pallets, big bags, and IBCs (bulk liquid containers) on the map. They are hidden from the Vehicles page and listed here.
economy.consumablesEmpty: No pallets, big bags, or IBCs found for this farm.
economy.consumablesBaleSummarySubtitle: Ground bales counted per farmland (same as the Fields tab), totaled for the selected farm.
economy.consumablesBaleInventorySubtitle: Counts by fill type for your farm: loose on cropland vs yards, sheds, and roads on your farmland.
economy.balesLooseOnFieldsHint: On the ground inside registered field boundaries (same logic as the Fields tab per-field count).
economy.balesInYardsShedsHint: Not credited to a field polygon — barn storage, yards, and farm lanes on your parcels.
economy.baleInventoryEmptyAll: No physical bales detected for your farm in this scan.
economy.baleInventoryLegacyHint: Update the FarmDashboard mod for per-type counts. Until then: {{total}} bale(s) on {{fields}} field(s) from field totals only.
economy.consumablesBaleSummaryZero: No bales counted on owned fields.
pastures.title: Pasture Management
pastures.subtitle: Monitor pastures, livestock, and grazing conditions
productions.title: Productions
productions.subtitleEmpty: No production chains reported for your farm yet, or the mod has not written fresh data.
productions.hintEmpty: Ensure the Farm Dashboard mod is active and production points exist on the map.
productions.noSlotData: No per-slot data from the game.
settings.serverManagerIntro: Same options as first-run setup: manage saves, FTP polling, and tools. Use Save to apply; open the full setup window if you prefer the dedicated screen.
settings.sectionsHint: Uncheck a section to hide it from the home screen. You can turn it back on here anytime.
settings.fieldExclusionHint: Only parcels your selected farm owns are listed. Hide any that still appear as fields after a pasture or farmyard. Switch farm in the header and reopen Settings for another farm.
settings.fieldExclusionEmpty: No owned field parcels to configure for this farm.
settings.fieldClusterHint: Automatic merge joins adjacent parcels with the same crop when the game sends field outlines. Manual lines override: one merged card per line, farmland numbers separated by commas (e.g. 12,13,14,15).
settings.fieldClusterEmpty: No owned parcels to configure for this farm.
settings.fieldClusterAuto: Auto-merge adjacent same-crop fields (when outlines exist)
settings.simHubHint: SimHub’s in-game web view cannot click the dashboard. This page shows only the block you pick — not the top navigation. It tracks the server and farm you last picked in Farm Dashboard on this PC.
settings.simHubClustersLabel: Field cluster ids (optional, one per line)
settings.simHubClustersHelp: Leave empty to list every field card. Use the cluster id shown on each merged field card (data-cluster-id).
settings.simHubUrl: Open on this PC or a tablet (optional ?serverId=&farmId= overrides):
settings.desktopAppHint: The Farm Dashboard installer checks GitHub Releases for new versions. FS25 mod updates use Giants Mod Hub or in-game mods.
updater.dialogDetail: Restart now to install? Your dashboard settings and farm data stay on this PC.
settings.lanIntro: Turn on LAN access below — until then, phones and tablets cannot use http://<this-PC-LAN-IP>:8766 (the server listens on this PC only). After enabling, open that URL from your tablet on the same Wi‑Fi. This app still uses 127.0.0.1 locally without a password. You may need an inbound Windows Firewall rule for TCP 8766. Changing these options restarts the built-in web server.
settings.lanEnableLabel: Enable LAN access (listen on all interfaces)
settings.lanAllowedIpsHelp: Leave empty to allow any LAN IP (still requires username and password). If set, only listed IPs may connect remotely.
settings.lanAuthOptional: Allow LAN read-only pages without username/password (GET only)
settings.lanAuthOptionalHint: Optional login skips Basic auth only for static pages and non-sensitive GET routes; live data, WebSocket tokens, and setup JSON still require credentials or loopback. Dangerous on public Wi‑Fi — use only on a trusted home LAN.
settings.lanAuthOptionalWarn: Optional LAN login turns off HTTP Basic for normal GET pages (not WebSockets). Anyone on your LAN could read dashboard farm data without a password. Enable only on a closed trusted network. Continue?
settings.lanTimeoutTroubleshoot: Other devices time out? If this PC can open its own LAN IP but phones or tablets never load the page, that is almost always the Wi‑Fi/router: use the same network (not guest/IoT), turn off AP / client isolation if your router has it, and check both devices use the same subnet (e.g. 192.168.1.x). Some VPN or security suites block device-to-device traffic even when Windows Firewall is off.
settings.modHint: These options match the in-game mod’s config file. Restart the game or reload the save for changes to apply. Applies to the default Documents mod path on this computer.
settings.modOneDriveHint: This PC may store FS25 under Documents, under OneDrive, under the Microsoft Store / Xbox app package path (AppData\Local\Packages\…\LocalCache\Local), or on another drive (WpSystem\…\Packages). The dashboard scans these locations and prefers a folder that already has mod data or saves. Custom mod paths from gameSettings.xml (modsDirectoryOverride) are followed when present. For stubborn sync issues, pause cloud sync while playing or relocate data via gameSettings.xml.
settings.sectionHidden: That section is turned off in Settings → Dashboard.
folder.title: Oops — something went wrong
folder.p1: We couldn’t connect to the dashboard API. Check that the game is running with the mod, or go back to Home to add or change server settings and browse local saves.
folder.p2: If this keeps happening, try restarting start-dashboard.bat or your server.
landing.title: Farm Management Dashboard
landing.lead: Select a section below to manage your farm data
card.livestockDesc: Manage your animals, track health, breeding, and locations
card.vehiclesDesc: View your fleet, maintenance status, and operating times
card.fieldsDesc: Monitor crop growth, soil conditions, and field management
card.economyDesc: Track commodity prices, market trends, and finances
card.pasturesDesc: Manage pastures, livestock, and grazing conditions
card.productionsDesc: Production chains, storage fill levels, and active slots
livestock.heading: Livestock Management
notif.title: Notification History
export.title: Export Livestock Data
export.intro: Choose your preferred export format for the livestock data:
farmSelect.title: Select Farm
farmSelect.body: Multiple farms found in this save file. Please select which farm's data you want to view:
farmSelect.footer: You can change this selection later from the dashboard
refresh.title: Refresh Farm Data
refresh.lead: Get Latest Data from Save Files
refresh.warn: Ensure to save in-game before loading data.
refresh.paragraph: To refresh with the latest data from your save files, you need to re-select the save folder. This allows the browser to read the updated files from disk.
refresh.whyLine: Browsers cannot automatically re-read files from disk for security reasons.
refresh.reselectSub: Get fresh data with latest changes

setup.langHint: Choose your language first. This matches the Windows installer step and the in-dashboard setting.
setup.emptyServers:
No servers added yet.

Use Auto-Detect or add one manually →
setup.ftpPollHint: Applies to dedicated (FTP) servers. Local saves use file watching, not this schedule.
setup.scheduleSync: Each cycle: poll all FTP servers at once
setup.scheduleStaggered: Staggered: one FTP server per equal slice across the interval
setup.modImages: Scan FS25 mods for dashboard images
setup.modImagesHint: Copies shop-style PNGs and converts icon_*.dds from your FS25 mods folder into the dashboard (items_mod_extract). DDS conversion uses ImageMagick if installed, or texconv.exe placed in the app resources/texconv folder.
setup.localHint: Leave blank to use the default Documents path.
setup.saveHint: Leave blank to use the Display Name as the folder name.
setup.slotHint: Which slot the mod writes data.json into.
setup.httpHint: If your dedicated server exposes the Giants HTTP feed, enter the details below. Provides vehicle age, prices and crop price history. Leave blank to use Lua-only mode.
apiStorage.confirmClearSavedFolder: Are you sure you want to clear the saved folder data?
apiStorage.confirmUnloadAll: Are you sure you want to unload all farm data?
apiStorage.toastCouldNotLoadData: Could not load data for this save. Check that the server has data or try again.
apiStorage.toastServerSetupPcOnly: Server and save setup is only available on the PC running Farm Dashboard.
changes.toastFoodDrop: Food dropped by {{drop}}L in {{pasture}} — check feed supply.
modExport.initSummary: Found {{folders}} mod folder(s) and {{zips}} zip archive(s) — {{steps}} step(s) to process.
setup.toastAutoDetectPcOnly: Auto-detect only runs on the PC with Farming Simulator saves. Use the Farm Dashboard app on that computer, or add a server manually below.
setup.toastNoDataJson: No data.json under FS25_FarmDashboard. Load a save in-game with this mod enabled.{{extra}}
setup.toastAllDetected: All detected saves are already in your list.
setup.toastModScanPcOnly: Mod image scan runs on the Windows PC with the game installed (desktop app only).
setup.toastFtpRequired: FTP Host, Username and Password are all required.
setup.toastAddAtLeastOne: Add at least one server first.
pastures.warnNoFoodData: Critical: No food data available for {{count}} animals - check feed levels immediately
pastures.warnAnimalsRequireFood: Animals require food and water. Game shows 0L - immediate attention needed.
pastures.warnNoWaterData: Critical: No water data available for {{count}} animals - check water supply immediately
pastures.warnAnimalsRequireWater: Animals require fresh water. Ensure water systems are functioning.
pastures.warnBreedingRatio: Breeding ratio: 1 male to {{ratio}} females is expected
pastures.warnDairySeparate: {{mothers}} lactating mothers with {{offspring}} young animals - separate for optimal milk production
pastures.details.title: {{name}} - Details
pastures.details.prodDataUnavailableBody: This pasture's production and food levels are not being monitored in real-time. This can happen when:
pastures.details.prodReasonRL: The RealisticLivestock mod is not monitoring this building
pastures.details.prodReasonNoMonitoring: The building doesn't have detailed monitoring enabled
pastures.details.prodReasonNew: The building is new and hasn't generated data yet
pastures.details.prodEstimatesNote: Values shown are estimates based on animal count and type.
pastures.livestockTable.noLivestockBody: No animals found in the selected pasture(s).
rules.cropGrowingWatchHud: Crop is growing and no soil job is flagged here right now — watch the in-field HUD for the next pass and plan the harvest window.
rules.action.rollFirstStage: Roll — first growth stage / seedbed finish
rules.action.spreadLimeEmerged: Spread lime (emerged crop — pre-drill is ideal next season)
rules.action.sprayHerbicide: Spray herbicide — weeds past mechanical window
rules.action.addNTarget: Add ~{{n}} kg N/ha to reach target
rules.action.topUpNitrogen: Top up nitrogen for this growth stage
rules.action.fertilizeStep: Fertilize — spray / nutrient step {{fl}}/2 (below full for this stage)
rules.action.pickStones: Pick stones or roll to bury them
rules.action.finishFlaggedSoilWork: Do the next soil pass (lime, fertiliser, weeds, roll, or plough)
rules.action.cultivateReseed: Start over: cultivate, then re-seed this parcel
rules.action.mowGrass: Mow or collect — grass is ready to cut
rules.action.baleStrawForage: Bale loose straw or pick up with a forage wagon
rules.action.tedderOrBale: Tedder to make hay, or bale wet and wrap for silage
rules.action.baleHay: Bale hay or collect dry forage
rules.action.clearAllWindrows: Clear loose straw, grass, and hay
rules.action.clearLooseForage: Clear loose forage on the field
rules.action.clearStrawGrassOrHay: Clear loose straw, grass, or hay
rules.action.baleStrawWagon: Bale straw or pick it up with a forage wagon
rules.action.tedDryHay: Ted dry hay or bale before weather hits
rules.action.finishGrass: Finish grass: tedder for hay, or merge and bale for silage
rules.action.pickupSwath: Pick up or bale the swathed crop
rules.action.clearSwath: Clear the swath (baler or forage wagon)
rules.action.moveOneBale: Move 1 bale off this field first
rules.action.moveBales: Move {{n}} bales off this field first
rules.action.runSoilScan: Run a soil scan on this field
rules.action.cultivateMulch: Cultivate to work in mulched stubble
rules.action.buildSoilN: Build soil N (manure/slurry or bag) before you drill
rules.action.fertilizeForDrilling: Fertilize — spray / nutrient level is low for drilling
rules.action.sowPlant: Sow or plant your next crop
rules.action.cultivateMulchedDrill: Cultivate mulched stubble, then drill or plant the next crop
rules.action.ploughBeforeNextCrop: Plough or deep-cultivate — primary tillage before the next crop
rules.action.ploughThenSeedbed: Plough or deep-cultivate, then finish the seedbed
rules.action.cultivateStubbleDrill: Cultivate stubble, then drill or plant the next crop
rules.action.limePastureMap: Lime pasture only if the pH map shows a real gap
rules.action.checkPasturePH: Check pasture pH before liming mown grass
rules.action.sowSeedbed: Sow — seedbed is worked and empty
rules.action.prepareDrill: Prepare soil, then drill or plant
rules.action.fallback: Use in-game field hints — saved data doesn’t show one clear job
rules.reason.limeBeforeSeedPF: pH is about {{ph}} vs ~{{target}} target — spread lime on prepared soil, then drill seed (not after seeding).
rules.reason.limeBeforeSeedNoPF: pH is below target for the next crop — lime before you drill or plant seed, so pH is right from emergence.
rules.reason.rollFirstStage: Rolling is not finished for this crop yet — roll once early for seed-to-soil contact and to press down small stones before later passes.
rules.reason.limeEmergedPF: {{label}}: pH ~{{ph}} vs target ~{{target}} — this is the tightest gap to fix first on the scan.
rules.reason.limeEmergedNoPF: {{label}}: pH is low — lime is the priority fix before chasing other passes.
rules.reason.weedsEarlyMechanical: Early growth (stage {{gs}}) — weeds are the worst issue for {{label}} right now; mechanical pass before herbicide.
rules.reason.weedsHerbicide: Growth stage {{gs}} — weed pressure on {{label}} is the priority pass now.
rules.reason.fertilizerPFGap: Mapped N {{n}} / {{target}} kg N/ha ({{pct}}% of target) — {{label}} is shortest on N versus the map; do this pass before other tweaks.
rules.reason.fertilizerSimple: {{label}} is lowest on fertilization for this stage — one nutrient pass is the next job.
rules.reason.pickStones: Stone level is up — walk the headlands slowly to spot rocks, then use a stone picker or collector on problem rows; a heavy roller can help sink small stones before drilling. Clear obstacles so drills and headers are not blocked.
rules.reason.finishFlaggedSoilWork: This field is still marked as needing work. Apply the highest-impact remaining pass first (lime, fertiliser, weed control, rolling, or ploughing), then recheck the card for the next step.
rules.reason.witheredGeneric: The standing crop has withered — till the soil and plant something new.
rules.reason.witheredCrop: Your {{label}} has withered — till the soil and plant something new.
rules.reason.grassReadyMow: Grass has reached cutting stage — bale silage/hay, wrap, or pick up before it gets rank.
rules.reason.combineHarvest: {{label}} is at harvest stage — get it off the field before rain, lodging, or withering costs yield.
rules.reason.looseStrawField: Loose straw on the field — bale or collect before tillage or the next pass.
rules.reason.freshGrassWindrow: Fresh-cut grass on the field — tedder or bale before the next stage.
rules.reason.hayWindrowField: Hay is on the field — bale or collect before the next stage.
rules.reason.allWindrowsField: Loose straw, grass, and hay are on the ground — clear forage before continuing.
rules.reason.strawGrassWindrows: Loose straw and grass are on the field — bale or collect before continuing.
rules.reason.strawHayField: Straw and hay on the field — bale or collect before continuing.
rules.reason.grassHayWindrows: Grass and hay are on the field — finish before the next stage.
rules.reason.looseForageGeneric: Loose forage material detected — bale or collect before the next field stage.
rules.reason.looseStrawGround: Loose straw on the ground — bale or collect before tillage or the next pass.
rules.reason.grassWindrowField: Grass is lying on the field — tedder or bale before the next stage.
rules.reason.hayWindrowSimple: Hay is on the field — bale or collect before the next stage.
rules.reason.multipleWindrows: Multiple forage types are lying on the field — clear them before the next stage.
rules.reason.strawDensityMap: Loose straw is on the field (density map) — bale or collect before tillage or the next crop.
rules.reason.strawCerealResidue: Loose straw or cereal residue is on the field — clear it so cultivation and the next crop aren’t blocked.
rules.reason.dryHayWindrow: Hay is on the ground — finish tedding if needed, then bale or store before rain.
rules.reason.freshGrassCut: Fresh-cut grass is still on the ground — dry/ted for hay, or bale for silage (wrap wet bales) before you till.
rules.reason.cropSwath: Crop windrows are on the field — combine pick-up, baler, or loader work before you work the soil.
rules.reason.strawCerealLying: Loose straw is lying on the field — clear it so cultivation and the next crop aren’t blocked.
rules.reason.swathCovering: Windrow or swath is covering the soil — remove it before you work ground for the next pass.
rules.reason.oneBale: There is 1 bale still on this farmland — clear it so cultivators and planters aren’t blocked.
rules.reason.manyBales: There are {{n}} bales still on this farmland — load or stack them out of the way before tillage or drilling.
rules.reason.runSoilScan: Scan data is needed before pH, nitrogen maps, and drilling advice match this parcel.
rules.reason.mulchBeforePlough: Field data says this parcel still needs ploughing — shred harvest residue first (not for grass reseeding).
rules.reason.ploughDeep: Primary tillage is still due on this parcel before you can build a clean seedbed — invert or loosen soil deep enough for your next crop.
rules.reason.cultivateMulch: Stubble is mulched — mix it in for an even seedbed. Lime (if needed) should already be planned before seed.
rules.reason.buildSoilNPF: Target ~{{target}} kg N/ha — you’re near {{n}}; add organic first if you use it, then mineral.
rules.reason.fertilizeForDrilling: Spray / nutrient step isn’t full — build N after lime, before you drill seed (or in an early pass right after drilling if that’s your routine).
rules.reason.optionalOrganicFirst: Use slurry/manure now if that is your route. If not, skip this optional step for this field cycle.
rules.reason.directDrillMulched: With Precision Farming on, this parcel is ready to plant — one pass with the drill through mulch/residue is the next job.
rules.reason.directDrillBare: With Precision Farming on, this parcel is ready to plant — one pass with the drill is the next job; skip extra cultivation if your setup allows.
rules.reason.sowPlant: Soil prep for this field is satisfied for drilling — the next job is putting seed in.
rules.reason.directDrillNoCrop: Precision Farming is on and there’s no standing crop — the next job is planting with a direct drill; skip a full cultivation pass if your setup allows.
rules.reason.cultivateMulchedDrill: Stubble is mulched and there’s no active crop — shallow cultivation before drilling is the usual path without Precision Farming maps guiding no-till.
rules.reason.mulchPostHarvest: Harvest residue is still standing and primary tillage is due — shred stubble so the plough or deep cultivator can work cleanly.
rules.reason.ploughBeforeNextCrop: Precision Farming is enabled and this parcel still needs full inversion or deep tillage — maps and flags say ploughing isn’t satisfied yet.
rules.reason.directDrillPostHarvest: Precision Farming is on and ploughing isn’t flagged — harvest is done, so the next job is planting with a direct drill and keeping tillage minimal.
rules.reason.ploughThenSeedbed: Harvest is done and the save still flags primary tillage — work the soil deep, then cultivate and drill (or follow your rotation’s cultivation step).
rules.reason.cultivateStubbleDrill: Harvest is done and ploughing isn’t flagged — shallow cultivation before the next seeding matches this field state (use in-game no-till if you prefer).
rules.reason.limePastureMapPF: Mapped pH ~{{ph}} vs target ~{{target}} — grass tolerates a wider band than arable; lime after cuts only when the scan says pH is short, not as a drill-window task.
rules.reason.checkPasturePH: Low lime flags on permanent grass often follow mowing — correct pH gradually; avoid treating pasture like pre-drill arable land.
rules.reason.buildSoilNTilled: Target ~{{target}} kg N/ha — you’re near {{n}}; finish after lime, before seed.
rules.reason.fertilizeTilledSimple: Bring spray/N up after lime and before drilling seed.
rules.reason.sowSeedbed: Ground reads cultivated/plowed with no crop — drill seed only after any required lime and starter N prep for this field.
rules.reason.prepareDrill: Field reads empty — plough/cultivate as needed, then put a crop in.
rules.reason.fallback: The offline rules don’t match this odd state — use in-game field hints or the live suggestions list from your save.
simhub.noFieldRows: No field rows for this farm.
